name: Tenkai Stone (the Tenkai Fortress key)
grants: Finding and opening the Tenkai Fortress — 'What good's the Fortress without the key?'

custody.how: Her father's parting gift in a fancy box, kept for years without knowing what it was; realized as the Fortress key at Vilius's ep-34 briefing and kept secret.
custody.evidence: analysis--arc-3 <!--ep:34-->: 'the shiny bling thing her dad left her'; ep34: 'Not if I happen to find it first.'

custody.how: Given to Kiiro at the park (ep 46) to carry to Guren — 'I've decided he deserves it.' Kiiro forgets to deliver it for a few days.
custody.evidence: ep46: 'Can you give this to Guren the next time you see him?'

custody.how: Kiiro finally delivers it to Guren (ep 48); the Knights take it to Quarton and unseal the Tenkai Fortress with it, ahead of Vilius.
custody.evidence: ep48: Kiiro: 'The Tenkai Stone! Beni gave that to you.'; Guren: 'I unsealed it.'

rules.evidence: analysis--arc-4 <!--ep:44-->: 'What good's the Fortress without the key?'; analysis--arc-5 <!--ep:48-->: Stone reaches Guren, then the Fortress is opened

name: The Guardians (who commands them, and their transporter)
grants: Interdimensional transport at Guardian scale — including deploying agents to Earth

custody.how: The four Guardians govern themselves through the ep-26 intervention — their last free act before the resurrection disaster.
custody.evidence: analysis--arc-1 <!--ep:1-->; ep26: 'We, the Guardians, have voted to intervene.'

custody.how: They revived his body to punish him (ep 27); his never-departed Tenkai energy overthrew them — corrupted black Guardians and their transporter served him through the finale run.
custody.evidence: ep27: 'Fools, it's only my physical form you revived. My Tenkai energy never left.'

custody.how: Vilius absorbs them for a last power surge in the finale, then falls; his defeat frees them, restored and self-governing again — 'we must remain vigilant.'
custody.evidence: ep51: 'This is how he repays us for serving him!' → after: 'As guardians, we must remain vigilant.'

rules.evidence: analysis--arc-3 <!--ep:27-->: 'with Vilius controlling the corrupted Guardians he gains their transporter — which is how Granox and Slyger reach Earth'

name: Black Dragon Key
grants: Full control of the Tenkai Dragon — dark twin of the white Key

custody.how: Hidden inside a tenkai seal in Benham Tower, the building where Guren's father works; undetected until the ep-22 triangulation ping.
custody.evidence: ep22: 'It's in Benham Tower.'; analysis--arc-2: 'The Key sits sealed in Benham Tower … inside a tenkai seal'

custody.how: Unsealed on the tower roof by the Knights' core bricks (ep 23, night of day 44), then seized by Dromus under Eurus's time-freeze.
custody.evidence: ep23: Gen: 'This belongs to me. Thanks for holding it.'

custody.how: Seized from the mutinous Dromus at the battle's start and used to wake the Dragon dark — then stripped by the Guardians' intervention within the same day.
custody.evidence: ep25: 'You hold only the means to power.'; ep26: 'Not for long! The key!' / 'Now you have nothing, Vilius!'

custody.how: Brought together with the White Key to restore and re-cube the Dragon (ep 26); its resting place afterward is never shown.
custody.evidence: ep26: 'Now bring the two keys together…'

name: Dragon Key (white)
grants: Opening the united Dragon Cubes — awakening the Tenkai Dragon

custody.how: Found in ep 12 (Guren carries it), used in the ep-13 awakening, and lost in that battle ('I lost the key' — ep 14 recap). Spans will be split when arc-1 episode days are authored (Phase 4).
custody.evidence: analysis--arc-1 <!--ep:12-->: 'The Dragon Key is found (Guren carries it)'; ep14 [quarton] Guren: 'Because you see, I lost the key.'

name: The five Dragon Cubes
grants: United and opened with the Dragon Key, they awaken the Tenkai Dragon

custody.how: Won one by one across arc 1 (days per the arc-1 one-day-per-episode anchor assumption).
custody.evidence: analysis--arc-1 <!--ep:2-->: 'split into five Dragon Cubes scattered across Quarton'

custody.how: Combined in ep 13; existence continues as the Dragon (see tenkai-dragon) through capture, dark revival, and restoration.
custody.evidence: analysis--arc-1 <!--ep:13-->: 'the five cubes combine'

custody.holder: Split white/black — good energy in the five Knights, evil cubes combined into Vilius
custody.how: The ep-26 forced convergence split the Dragon in two (revealed ep 27): the white cubes' good energy is infused in the five Knights; all the evil cubes were combined with Vilius.
custody.evidence: ep27: Mr. White: 'The Dragon was essentially split in two, into good and evil, white and black Dragon Cubes.'

name: Tenkai Dragon
grants: The mightiest force on Quarton — its power belongs to whoever holds it

custody.how: Exists only as the five scattered Dragon Cubes until the ep-13 awakening (day per the arc-1 one-day-per-episode anchor assumption).
custody.evidence: analysis--arc-1 <!--ep:2-->: 'split into five Dragon Cubes scattered across Quarton'

custody.how: Awakened ep 13 and immediately captured by Dromus's trap; held inside a containment sphere spun from its own energy while Dromus taps its power.
custody.evidence: analysis--arc-1 <!--ep:13-->: 'the Dragon is captured, transported to the Corrupted headquarters'; ep14: 'The beast has built its own cage.'

custody.how: Revived DARK by the Black Key and ridden into battle — immobilized by the Guardians and restored good by both keys within the same day.
custody.evidence: ep25: 'Awaken, Dragon! Vilius commands you!'; ep26: 'Forgive us, Dragon, for what we must do.'

custody.holder: Split in two — good half in the five Knights, evil half in Vilius
custody.how: The ep-26 forced convergence split the Dragon (revealed ep 27): good cubes' energy in the Knights, evil cubes combined into Vilius, whose power grows.
custody.evidence: ep27: 'The Dragon was essentially split in two, into good and evil, white and black Dragon Cubes.'

custody.how: Vilius revives the evil half as an evil Tenkai Dragon (ep 49) and corrupts the Knights' good dragon into a second (ep 50); both his, briefly. (Finale days are compressed/approximate.)
custody.evidence: ep49: 'Destroy them, evil dragon!'; ep50: 'Let the good be made evil!'

custody.how: The Tenkai Wolf breaks both evil dragons in the finale (ep 51); the Dragon Cubes return to the Knights.
custody.evidence: ep51: 'Got our Dragon Cubes back! … the wolf just pwned both Dragons!'

name: The Corrupted's Reassembly Device
grants: Repair and redeploy Corrupted fighters that have been damaged or even destroyed

custody.how: The chamber inside the fortress that rebuilds Vilius's fighters after every defeat — the reason Granox and Slyger never stay beaten.
custody.evidence: ep24: Beag: 'the Reassembly Device, which the Corrupted use to repair and redeploy fighters that have been damaged or even destroyed.'

custody.how: Wrecked by Valorn and Lydendor during the fortress assault (ep 25).
custody.evidence: ep25: 'You are currently standing in it. This chamber is the reassembly device.' / 'And you can't be reassembled. So there.'

name: Beni's warehouse (Transdimensional Portal)
grants: Her private door to Quarton, independent of Mr. White's shop

custody.how: Her territory — the warehouse four floors below her apartment building, shared operationally with Gen during their partnership.
custody.evidence: analysis--arc-2 <!--ep:18-->: 'leads them to *her* warehouse'; <!--ep:25-->: 'Beni's warehouse (with portal) is established as her territory (ep 18)'

custody.how: The hidden interdimensional portal's Earth side, inside his antiques shop.
custody.evidence: analysis--arc-1 <!--ep:1-->: 'A hidden interdimensional portal links the worlds; its Earth side sits inside Mr. White's Shop of Wonders'
